steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The terms $9x^2$ and $5x^2$ are like terms because they have the same variable part. The constant $6$ is not an $x^2$ term, so it stays separate instead of being added to the coefficient.

steps.title: Combine the coefficients
steps.content:
Add only the coefficients of the like terms. The exponent stays $2$ because adding like terms changes the coefficient, not the variable part.
$$9x^2+5x^2=14x^2$$
The expression becomes $14x^2+6$. The answer is B.
